target.title: feat(hooks): emit session prune lifecycle event
target.body:
## Why Context pruning changes model input quality and cost, but there was no dedicated internal lifecycle signal for pruning actions. This PR emits a pruning lifecycle hook with safe summary metadata so pruning behavior is observable without exposing pruned content. Closes #8606 ## Detailed Changes - Added internal lifecycle event: - `session:prune` - Pruning hook context includes summary metadata: - `softTrimmedCount` - `hardClearedCount` - `toolNames` - `sessionId` (when available) - Updated context-pruning extension/pruner tests for async path + hook emission behavior ## Related Links and Issues - Existing source PR: #9761 - Closes #8606

Greptile Overview

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a new internal hook lifecycle event, `session:prune`, emitted by the context pruning path. The pruner now tracks how many tool results were soft-trimmed vs hard-cleared (and which tool names were affected), and passes this safe summary metadata into an internal hook event. The context-pruning extension’s `context` handler was updated to be async to await pruning, and tests were updated to cover the async path and hook emission behavior. Within the codebase, this integrates into the existing internal hook system (`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`) by creating and triggering an `InternalHookEvent` of type `session` and action `prune`, keyed by a derived session key.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- This PR is mostly safe to merge, but there is one correctness issue around the emitted hook event timestamp semantics that should be clarified/fixed. - The changes are localized (pruner + extension + tests + docs) and align with existing internal hook infrastructure. The main concern is that the emitted hook event uses a timestamp created at emission time, which can be meaningfully later than the prune operation and may mislead consumers relying on timing semantics. - src/agents/pi-extensions/context-pruning/pruner.ts

target.title: fix(agents): suggest recovery for unknown tool ids
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Fixes #92273, where Tool Search `mode: "tools"` could leave a model stuck after guessing an id such as `file_write`; the unknown-id error was not actionable enough for the caller to recover before memory flush/compaction work completed.
- Solution: Keep unknown-id recovery guidance tied to the caller contract: raw Tool Search controls mention `tool_search` / `tool_describe` / `tool_call`, generic Code Mode mentions `tools.search` / `tools.describe` / `tools.call`, and the Tool Search Code Mode bridge mentions `openclaw.tools.search` / `openclaw.tools.describe` / `openclaw.tools.call`.
- What changed: Added a caller-specific recovery surface to the Tool Search lookup/formatter path, wired generic Code Mode to its `tools.*` surface, added `file_write` guessed-id regressions, preserved short no-suggestion direct misses, and normalized raw QuickJS runtime interrupts to the existing Code Mode timeout contract.
- **What did NOT change:** This does not add a new Tool Search public API, config option, schema field, migration, compatibility alias, guessed-id execution path, MCP namespace exposure, or broad tool-matching behavior. Existing raw Tool Search control names and Code Mode bridge method names remain unchanged.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** A recoverable unknown-tool error lets the model search, describe, and retry with the exact available tool before durable session memory is lost; Code Mode users no longer receive instructions for tool APIs that are unavailable inside their active caller surface.
- Fixes #92273

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A model or Code Mode script that guesses `file_write` when the actual catalog entry is `write` now receives a recoverable unknown-id error with the correct API surface for the caller. Raw Tool Search mode still receives raw control-tool guidance, generic Code Mode receives `tools.*` guidance, and the Tool Search Code Mode bridge receives `openclaw.tools.*` guidance.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw worktree on Linux at commit `7f80f29448cf85e1ebbe158a4c87748108f91025`. The proof commands import and execute production `applyToolSearchCatalog` / `createToolSearchTools` and generic Code Mode `applyCodeModeCatalog` / `createCodeModeTools` paths after the patch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-search.test.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-generic-code-mode-runtime-proof.mjs
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import { applyToolSearchCatalog, createToolSearchTools } from "./src/agents/tool-search.ts"; const resultText = (error) => error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error); const writeTool = { name: "write", label: "Write", description: "Write a file to the workspace", parameters: { type: "object", properties: { path: { type: "string" }, content: { type: "string" } } }, execute: async () => ({ content: [{ type: "text", text: "unexpected write" }], details: { called: true } }) }; const codeSession = { sessionId: "proof-code-guessed-file-write-current", sessionKey: "agent:main:main" }; const codeToolsForCatalog = createToolSearchTools({ ...codeSession, config: {} }); applyToolSearchCatalog({ tools: [codeToolsForCatalog[0], writeTool], config: { tools: { toolSearch: true } }, ...codeSession }); const [codeTool] = createToolSearchTools({ ...codeSession, config: {} }); try { await codeTool.execute("proof-code-mode", { code: "return await openclaw.tools.call(\"file_write\", { path: \"memory/2026-05-22.md\" });" }); } catch (error) { console.log("CODE_MODE_BRIDGE_ERROR=" + resultText(error)); } const rawSession = { sessionId: "proof-raw-guessed-file-write-current", sessionKey: "agent:main:main" }; const rawControlsForCatalog = createToolSearchTools({ ...rawSession, config: { tools: { toolSearch: { mode: "tools" } } } }); applyToolSearchCatalog({ tools: [rawControlsForCatalog[1], rawControlsForCatalog[2], rawControlsForCatalog[3], writeTool], config: { tools: { toolSearch: { mode: "tools" } } }, ...rawSession }); const rawTools = createToolSearchTools({ ...rawSession, config: { tools: { toolSearch: { mode: "tools" } } } }); try { await rawTools[3].execute("proof-raw-tools", { id: "file_write", args: { path: "memory/2026-05-22.md", content: "remember this" } }); } catch (error) { console.log("RAW_TOOL_CALL_ERROR=" + resultText(error)); }'
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
GENERIC_CODE_MODE_STATUS=completed
GENERIC_CODE_MODE_VALUE=Unknown tool id: file_write. Did you mean: write? Use tools.search to find a tool, tools.describe to inspect it, then tools.call with the exact id or name.

CODE_MODE_BRIDGE_ERROR=Error: Unknown tool id: file_write. Did you mean: write? Use openclaw.tools.search to find a tool, openclaw.tools.describe to inspect it, then openclaw.tools.call with the exact id or name.
RAW_TOOL_CALL_ERROR=Unknown tool id: file_write. Did you mean: write? Use tool_search to find a tool, tool_describe to inspect it, then tool_call with the exact id or name.

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93374

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  100 passed (100)
   Duration  33.46s (transform 23.48s, setup 2.13s, import 26.79s, tests 23.35s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 45.44s

git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
# passed: no output
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The same guessed `file_write` id now returns caller-appropriate recovery guidance in all three production runtime surfaces. Generic Code Mode no longer instructs the child process to use raw structured tool names; the raw Tool Search and Tool Search Code Mode bridge surfaces preserve their existing API names.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a full local gateway chat with a live model performing the pre-compaction memory-flush self-correction loop end-to-end. The current proof executes the deterministic production runtime paths that generate the model-visible recovery errors; if maintainers require the full provider/gateway compaction scenario before merge, that remains a live-proof boundary rather than a source-path gap.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Fixed on current head by adding the `src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` regression for `tools.call("file_write", ...)`; the requested Vitest shard now passes with 100 tests.
- **RF-002:** Fixed by wiring generic Code Mode to `tools.search` / `tools.describe` / `tools.call` recovery text; current-head generic Code Mode runtime proof prints the `tools.*` guidance.
- **RF-003:** Addressed as a proof boundary. Current-head proof now covers the deterministic production lookup, raw Tool Search, Tool Search Code Mode bridge, and generic Code Mode paths, but not the full live model-driven pre-compaction memory flush loop.
- **RF-004:** Fixed with the narrow PR-branch repair requested by ClawSweeper: generic Code Mode `tools.*` recovery guidance plus the missing focused regression.
- **RF-005:** Fixed at the shared formatter/lookup boundary by selecting recovery text from the active caller surface before returning the unknown-id error.
- **RF-006:** Fixed by rerunning `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-search.test.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` after dependency hydration; the shard passed with 2 files and 100 tests.
- **RF-007:** Fixed by rerunning `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`; it passed with no output.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test files:** `src/agents/tool-search.test.ts` for raw Tool Search and Tool Search Code Mode bridge guessed-id behavior, plus `src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` for generic Code Mode `tools.call("file_write", ...)` recovery guidance and Code Mode timeout normalization.
- **Scenario locked in:** Code Mode calls `tools.call("file_write", ...)`, the catalog contains `write`, and the error must suggest `write` plus generic Code Mode `tools.*` recovery APIs. Existing Tool Search coverage locks the raw `tool_call` and `openclaw.tools.call` guessed-id paths.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression reaches the production catalog/bridge behavior that constructs the model-visible error while avoiding aliases, silent guessed-tool execution, or broad end-to-end live-model coupling.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-search.test.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts
# passed: 2 files, 100 tests

git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
# passed: no output
```

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** availability, automation, security-boundary, auth-provider, public contract, compatibility/default behavior.
- **Risk explanation:** The merge risk is limited to error text for unknown tool ids with suggestions and timeout classification for raw QuickJS runtime interrupts. Public contract surface was reviewed because `tool_search`, `openclaw.tools.*`, and `tools.*` are caller-visible API strings, but this patch preserves the existing canonical APIs and changes only which already-existing recovery path is named for each caller.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff is focused, has caller-surface regressions plus production runtime proof, and keeps compatibility/default behavior stable: raw Tool Search callers still receive raw control-tool instructions, Code Mode callers still use documented Code Mode APIs, no config/schema/protocol migration is introduced, and out-of-scope tool execution behavior is intentionally left unchanged.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The shared unknown-tool formatter originally had one recovery instruction string even though OpenClaw exposes Tool Search through multiple caller contracts. Structured Tool Search controls must mention raw control tools, but generic Code Mode and the Tool Search Code Mode bridge cannot call those same names from inside their child process surfaces. A required-suite failure also exposed a Code Mode timeout contract boundary: raw QuickJS runtime `interrupted` errors can escape through the worker top-level catch for async hostile loops, causing the host to see `internal_error` / `interrupted` instead of the runtime timeout contract.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix moves recovery guidance selection to the lookup/formatter boundary that owns the caller contract, so the model-visible error is correct before it is returned to the raw Tool Search or Code Mode caller. It also normalizes raw QuickJS runtime interrupts at the worker/host boundary where the timeout contract is defined, while preserving guest-thrown `Error("interrupted")` as an internal guest error.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the source/runtime path; current HEAD has fresh runtime proof, the requested focused Vitest shard, `git diff --check`, and regressions for the reviewed Code Mode failure modes. The only disclosed gap is the full live provider/gateway memory-flush loop.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings are expected from caller-contract text, timeout normalization, and merge-risk keywords. They are not symptom masking: the formatter now receives the canonical caller surface before constructing the returned error, and the timeout change preserves the existing Code Mode timeout contract for raw VM interrupts rather than changing retry behavior or hiding execution failures.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth owner remains the caller surface contract. Raw structured Tool Search uses raw control tool names; generic Code Mode uses `tools.*`; the Tool Search Code Mode bridge uses `openclaw.tools.*`. This patch does not change public config, schema, protocol, migration, default behavior, or compatibility aliases.

target.title: fix(memory): fall back to keyword search without sqlite
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: On Node runtimes where the builtin `node:sqlite` module is unavailable, `memory_search` previously lost the builtin indexed manager even though durable markdown memory files were still readable.
- Solution: Keep SQLite/vector search as the canonical path when available, and only for the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` failure seam return a degraded keyword fallback manager over `MEMORY.md`, `memory/*.md`, and configured memory `extraPaths`.
- What changed: The fallback returns bounded markdown keyword results with file paths, line ranges, truncated snippets, and debug/status metadata marking `keyword-fallback` / `node-sqlite-unavailable`.
- What did not change: SQLite/vector indexing, QMD behavior, embeddings, provider selection, sync, runtime service APIs, tool schemas, SDK exports, config shape, migrations, and file formats are unchanged.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users on Node builds without `node:sqlite` get a basic local memory recall path instead of an unavailable manager response for a runtime capability gap outside their control.
- Fixes #93150

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `memory_search` returns degraded markdown keyword results when the current Node process cannot load `node:sqlite`, instead of returning unavailable manager metadata. The proof exercises the current PR's production `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts` path and a real markdown memory corpus, not a mocked Vitest seam.
- **Real environment tested:** Local #93260 worktree at commit `96d9ef309aead772a74e68e9aaece8021f94f7e2`; git status clean; Node `v22.22.0`; same Node process launched with `--no-experimental-sqlite` and `--import tsx`; isolated local memory workspace containing `MEMORY.md` and `memory/preferences.md`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93260
TASK_WORKTREE=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93260 \
EVIDENCE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93260-evidence \
node --no-experimental-sqlite --import tsx \
  /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93260-evidence/no-node-sqlite-runtime-proof.mts
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
# no-node:sqlite memory_search runtime proof
{
  "behaviorAddressed": "memory_search falls back to markdown keyword results when node:sqlite is unavailable",
  "head": "96d9ef309aead772a74e68e9aaece8021f94f7e2",
  "gitStatusShort": "clean",
  "nodeVersion": "v22.22.0",
  "execArgv": [
    "--no-experimental-sqlite",
    "--import",
    "tsx"
  ],
  "sqliteProbe": "No such built-in module: node:sqlite",
  "runtimeDebug": [
    {
      "backend": "builtin",
      "effectiveMode": "keyword-fallback",
      "fallback": "node-sqlite-unavailable"
    }
  ],
  "providerStatus": {
    "backend": "builtin",
    "provider": "keyword-fallback",
    "requestedProvider": "keyword-fallback",
    "sources": [
      "memory"
    ],
    "fallback": {
      "from": "builtin-sqlite",
      "reason": "SQLite support is unavailable in this Node runtime (missing node:sqlite). No such built-in module: node:sqlite | No such built-in module: node:sqlite | ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE"
    }
  },
  "resultCount": 2,
  "results": [
    {
      "path": "memory/preferences.md",
      "startLine": 1,
      "endLine": 4,
      "score": 1,
      "textScore": 1,
      "snippet": "# Preferences\nFavorite ramen is shoyu.\nThe orbit codename is ORBIT-22.\n",
      "source": "memory"
    },
    {
      "path": "MEMORY.md",
      "startLine": 1,
      "endLine": 3,
      "score": 0.6,
      "textScore": 0.6,
      "snippet": "# Root Memory\nThe orbit launch window is morning.\n",
      "source": "memory"
    }
  ]
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The same process could not load `node:sqlite`, `getMemorySearchManager()` returned a builtin `keyword-fallback` manager, `search("orbit ramen")` emitted fallback debug metadata, and the search returned markdown memory results including `memory/preferences.md` with the `ORBIT-22` snippet.
- **What was not tested:** A separate Node 20 full-repo run was not used for this proof because this checkout's dependency stack requires newer Node APIs; the proof instead uses Node 22's runtime flag to make `node:sqlite` unavailable in the actual process that imports and runs the PR's production memory manager. Full gateway/UI behavior, semantic/vector ranking parity, session transcript search, QMD, embeddings, provider calls, and live service APIs were not tested because this PR changes the memory-core manager-resolution fallback seam.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Fixed. The proof above is redacted after-fix terminal output from a real Node process where `require("node:sqlite")` fails with `No such built-in module: node:sqlite`, and it shows `memory_search` manager lookup returning fallback markdown results.
- **RF-002:** Fixed. The PR body no longer relies only on mocked regression tests and CI output; it includes the no-`node:sqlite` runtime command, runtime flags, provider status, debug metadata, and returned markdown search results.
- **RF-003:** Disclosed for reviewer acceptance. The intentional compatibility change is that the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` path now returns degraded keyword result objects instead of disabled/unavailable metadata. Existing callers that keyed specifically on unavailable metadata may observe `keyword-fallback` status plus result rows; runtimes with `node:sqlite` available remain on the existing SQLite/vector path.
- **RF-004:** Fixed. The proof process itself reports `sqliteProbe: "No such built-in module: node:sqlite"` and `execArgv: ["--no-experimental-sqlite", "--import", "tsx"]`, then shows fallback markdown results.
- **RF-005:** Disclosed for maintainer decision. When SQLite is absent, the fallback scans configured markdown memory files on each search. That trades semantic/vector quality and indexed performance for degraded availability; the search remains local, source-scoped to memory files, bounded by `maxResults`, and snippet-truncated.
- **RF-006:** Human review remains appropriate for the policy choice, but the contributor proof gate is now supplied. Maintainers still need to accept the degraded keyword fallback semantics versus the previous unavailable-manager response shape.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** The regression forces `MemoryIndexManager.get()` to throw the wrapped missing-`node:sqlite` error, then verifies `getMemorySearchManager()` returns a keyword fallback manager, searches a real `MEMORY.md` / `memory/*.md` fixture, emits fallback debug metadata, preserves path/line/snippet metadata, and avoids a direct `fs.readFile` fallback corpus path.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The bug is at the memory-core manager-resolution seam, not in provider calls or gateway transport. The test isolates the exact SQLite initialization failure classification and the safe memory-file read boundary without requiring a live embedding provider or vector index.
- **Additional local verification:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`, and `git diff --check` passed earlier for this PR series.
- **Current-head CI:** GitHub checks are aligned to `96d9ef309aead772a74e68e9aaece8021f94f7e2`; CI, CodeQL Critical Quality, Dependency Guard, Real behavior proof, Workflow Sanity, OpenGrep PR Diff, iOS Periphery Dead Code, Labeler, Auto response, and ClawSweeper Dispatch are reported successful in `ci-checkout-context.json`.
- **New proof verification:** The no-`node:sqlite` runtime proof above was recorded against HEAD `96d9ef309aead772a74e68e9aaece8021f94f7e2` with clean git status.

## Patch quality notes

- **Patch quality notes:** The fallback wording in the diff is intentional and narrow: it names a degraded manager that only activates for errors classified as missing `node:sqlite`; other builtin manager setup failures still surface unavailable metadata instead of being hidden.
- **Default returns:** Empty-result returns are limited to normal no-match/no-memory-source cases inside the degraded fallback search. They do not swallow SQLite initialization failures outside the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` classifier.
- **Filesystem boundary:** Fallback corpus reads go through `readMemoryFile`, preserving the existing memory host boundary for workspace paths, configured `extraPaths`, regular-file checks, line limits, and character limits.
- **Related open PR scan:** I checked adjacent open PRs for `memory_search`, `node:sqlite`, and keyword fallback memory work; they cover adjacent ranking, timeout, messaging, and hybrid-keyword topics, but none is a broader canonical fix for this exact missing-`node:sqlite` manager-resolution fallback plus safe memory-file read boundary.
- **Scope boundary:** This PR only changes `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts` and its focused regression test. It does not alter provider routing, embedding calls, QMD, gateway transport, public tool schemas, public config schema, storage format, migrations, or runtime service APIs.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** SQLite/vector indexing remains the source-of-truth search path when available. The keyword manager is a compatibility fallback for a known runtime capability gap and delegates allowed corpus access to existing memory host helpers.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** Semantic/vector ranking parity, session transcript search, new public config, new schema fields, SDK export changes, migrations, provider/embedding behavior, QMD behavior, gateway/UI behavior, and live service APIs are intentionally out of scope.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: compatibility`, `merge-risk: availability`, `merge-risk: security-boundary`, `merge-risk: session-state`, plus parser-detected auth-provider/message-delivery signals from provider/status wording.
- **Risk explanation:** The compatibility risk is the intentional response-shape change for the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` path: callers now see `provider: "keyword-fallback"` and markdown result rows instead of unavailable-manager metadata. The availability risk is that fallback searches scan configured markdown memory files on each search. The security/session-state boundary is local memory-file access and memory result sourcing.
- **Why acceptable:** The changed behavior is limited to the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` runtime capability gap; runtimes with SQLite keep the existing indexed path. Fallback reads use `readMemoryFile`, results are source-scoped to memory files, bounded by `maxResults`, snippet-truncated, and marked with fallback debug/status metadata so callers can distinguish degraded keyword results from vector-backed search.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The builtin memory search manager treated the missing `node:sqlite` runtime capability as a generic terminal setup failure, even though the invariant for durable memory is that readable markdown memory should remain available through the memory host file boundary when the indexed backend is unavailable for a known runtime capability gap.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The manager-resolution seam now classifies the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` failure at its source and returns a degraded keyword fallback manager for that runtime capability gap, while leaving unrelated manager setup failures as unavailable errors. The fallback corpus is read through existing memory-file helpers and reports degraded mode in runtime debug/status metadata, so the fix changes the source manager selection behavior rather than masking one downstream call site.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high after the new no-`node:sqlite` runtime proof. The remaining decision is policy acceptance of degraded keyword fallback semantics and its compatibility/availability tradeoff.
- **Source-of-truth boundary:** SQLite/vector search remains canonical whenever `node:sqlite` is available. The fallback is not a new canonical ranking engine; it is a bounded compatibility path for markdown memory recall when the builtin SQLite dependency cannot load.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** Runtimes where `node:sqlite` is available keep the existing SQLite/vector search behavior. Only the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` path changes response shape from unavailable metadata to `keyword-fallback` status and markdown result rows.
- **Availability tradeoff:** The fallback favors bounded local availability over semantic/vector quality and indexed performance when SQLite is absent; maintainers should explicitly accept that degraded behavior before merge.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** Semantic/vector ranking parity, new public config, new schema fields, runtime service API changes, SDK export changes, migrations, provider/embedding behavior, QMD behavior, and session transcript search.

target.title: fix: resolve response prefix template variables in abort replies
target.body:
## Summary

Template variables are not populated when user issues early stop commands. 

- **Problem:** When a user sends a "stop" command (e.g. on WhatsApp), the abort reply displays unresolved template variables: `{model} - {thinkingLevel} 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`
- **Why it matters:** The raw `{model}` and `{thinkingLevel}` placeholders leak into user-facing messages, looking broken and confusing.
- **What changed:** `createReplyPrefixContext()` now pre-seeds the `ResponsePrefixContext` with model, provider, and thinkingLevel resolved from config defaults, so early-exit paths like abort have valid values for template interpolation.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to abort logic, dispatch flow, template resolution engine, or `formatAbortReplyText()`. The `onModelSelected()` callback still overwrites the defaults at runtime during normal replies.

## Change Type (select all)

- [X] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [X] Gateway / orchestration
- [X] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #45314

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Abort replies now display the resolved model name and thinking level from config (e.g. `kimi-k2.5 - off 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`) instead of literal `{model} - {thinkingLevel}` placeholders.
- Applies to all channels using a `responsePrefix` template with `{model}` or `{thinkingLevel}` variables.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+ / pnpm
- Model/provider: openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5
- Integration/channel: WhatsApp (reproduces on any channel with `responsePrefix` template)
- Relevant config:
  ```json
  {
    "messages": {
      "responsePrefix": "{model} - {thinkingLevel} 🦞: "
    }
  }
  ```

### Steps

1. Configure a `responsePrefix` with `{model}` and/or `{thinkingLevel}` template variables
2. Start a conversation so the agent is processing
3. Send "stop" or `/stop`

### Expected

- Reply: `kimi-k2.5 - off 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`

### Actual

- Reply: `{model} - {thinkingLevel} 🦞: ⚙️ Agent was aborted.`

## Evidence
Before
![before](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/73faefe5-65c3-488d-a429-94edf51ca959)
After
![after](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e70f899-9353-4679-a63a-4272a8d7d197)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `/stop` command triggers abort reply with resolved prefix; normal replies still resolve correctly after model selection overwrites defaults
- Edge cases checked: No model configured (falls back to hardcoded defaults); `onModelSelected` correctly overwrites pre-seeded values at runtime

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the single commit
- Files/config to restore: `src/channels/reply-prefix.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Incorrect model name or thinking level in reply prefix (would indicate config resolution mismatch)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `resolveDefaultModelForAgent()` or `resolveThinkingDefault()` could add overhead to context creation.
  - Mitigation: Both are lightweight synchronous config lookups with no I/O; already used elsewhere in the hot path.

---

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/channels/reply-prefix.ts` | Pre-seed `ResponsePrefixContext` with default model/provider/thinkingLevel from config |
| `src/channels/reply-prefix.test.ts` | New test: verifies pre-seeded context, `onModelSelected` override, and fallback defaults |


---

## Notes

- This PR is AI-assisted (fully tested: `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` — 919 test files, 2642 tests passing).
- Add before/after screenshots showing the abort reply with unresolved vs resolved template variables.
- The root cause is that the abort fast-path in `dispatch-from-config.ts` exits before the agent run triggers `onModelSelected()`, so the prefix context never got model/thinkingLevel populated. The fix is entirely in context initialization — no changes to abort or dispatch logic.

target.body:
Closes #93465.

Summary:
- avoid direct Windows .cmd/.bat/.ps1 adapter wrapper spawns from ACPX generated launchers
- report ACP session-init spawn failures with redacted gateway logging and actionable Windows guidance
- keep the touched extension check green by making bounded Codex thread config merging total
- refresh generated Codex prompt snapshots and current guard baselines for the rebased main

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: ACPX launch preparation rejects unresolved Windows PowerShell wrappers and generated Claude ACP launchers no longer contain the direct `npx.cmd` fallback that produced raw `spawn EINVAL` failures.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, running the real plugin SDK Windows spawn resolver and ACPX generated-wrapper path with `platform: "win32"` inputs.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx` created a temporary `npx.ps1`, called `resolveWindowsSpawnProgram` with Windows platform/PATHEXT inputs, then generated the Claude ACP wrapper with `prepareAcpxCodexAuthConfig`.

Evidence after fix: Console output from the live command:

```json
{
  "ps1WrapperRejected": true,
  "generatedWrapperHasWin32Guard": true,
  "generatedWrapperMentionsDisabledDirectNpx": true,
  "generatedWrapperDirectNpxCmdLiteral": false
}
```

Observed result after fix: The PowerShell wrapper path failed closed, and the generated launcher includes the Windows guard without a direct `npx.cmd` literal fallback.

What was not tested: Live Windows gateway launch was not tested on this macOS host.

target.title: fix(hooks): deliver internal hook replies on replyable surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `event.messages` was documented as a generic user-reply channel, but only `/new` and `/reset` actually drained those messages.
- Why it matters: `message:received` hooks could push reply text that was silently dropped, while other lifecycle hooks looked supported even though they had no safe reply target.
- What changed: added a shared internal hook reply-drain helper, kept reset/new command behavior, and now deliver `message:received` hook replies when a routable reply target exists.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not make bootstrap, preprocessed/transcribed, sent, or other lifecycle-only hooks auto-reply.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #39688
- Related #40185

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Internal hook replies pushed to `event.messages` are now delivered on `message:received` when OpenClaw has a reply-capable routing target.
- Docs now explicitly limit `event.messages` auto-delivery to reply-capable surfaces instead of implying it works for all hooks.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): internal hooks / inbound reply routing
- Relevant config (redacted): default hook + routeReply test harnesses

### Steps

1. Register an internal hook that pushes text into `event.messages`.
2. Trigger it from `/new` or `message:received`.
3. Verify the reply is routed only when a safe reply target exists.

### Expected

- Reply-capable surfaces route the accumulated hook messages.
- Lifecycle-only surfaces remain non-replying unless they gain an explicit delivery contract later.

### Actual

- Before this fix, `message:received` hook replies were silently dropped.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: `commands-core` still routes reset/new hook replies; `dispatch-from-config` now routes `message:received` hook replies on routable surfaces and skips non-routable ones.
- Edge cases checked: non-routable surfaces do not auto-send hook replies; docs/comments now match that contract.
- What you did **not** verify: live messaging-provider delivery outside the existing routeReply test harnesses.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert `fix(hooks): deliver internal hook replies on replyable surfaces`
- Files/config to restore: `src/auto-reply/reply/internal-hook-replies.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-core.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`, `docs/automation/hooks.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: duplicate or unexpected hook replies on inbound turns, or `message:received` hook replies not routing when they should.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `message:received` hook replies use the same route-reply path as other provider-routed responses, so a bad target selection would misroute the hook message.
  - Mitigation: the helper uses existing inbound routing fields (`OriginatingTo`, `To`, channel normalization) and adds explicit tests for routable and non-routable surfaces.
- Risk: this branch also carries a whitespace-only `CONTRIBUTING.md` formatter cleanup required for `pnpm check`.
  - Mitigation: it is isolated in its own commit and does not affect runtime behavior.

target.title: feat(agents): per-agent outbound A2A allowlist override
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `agents.list[].tools.agentToAgent.allow` to control which agents a specific agent may contact via session tools (`sessions_send`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, `session_status`)
- When unset, the agent falls back to the global `tools.agentToAgent.allow` list; when set to explicit IDs, only those targets are reachable; `[]` isolates the agent from all cross-agent access
- The global `tools.agentToAgent.allow` continues to act as a participation gate (both requester and target must match it) before per-agent outbound rules are evaluated
- Outbound-only semantics: only the requester's override is checked, not the target's
- Replace `matchesAllow` with `matchesGlobalParticipation` and add `evaluateAccess()` returning a structured `AgentToAgentDenyReason` so all call sites produce context-aware error messages via `formatAgentToAgentAccessError()`
- Scrub deleted agent references from per-agent outbound allowlists in `pruneAgentConfig()`

### Example config (hub-spoke pattern)

```json
{
  "tools": {
    "agentToAgent": { "enabled": true, "allow": ["*"] }
  },
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      { "id": "david",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["*"] } } },
      { "id": "spoke",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["david"] } } },
      { "id": "kelly",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["david"] } } }
    ]
  }
}
```

spoke→david ✅ | spoke→kelly ❌ | david→anyone ✅

## Test plan

- [x] `sessions-access.test.ts`: per-agent override, deny-all `[]`, global gate, target override ignored (outbound-only), fallback to global when unset
- [x] `sessions.test.ts`: per-agent outbound deny during label lookup in `sessions_send`
- [x] `openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`: per-agent outbound deny for cross-agent `session_status`
- [x] `agents.test.ts`: `pruneAgentConfig` scrubs deleted agent from per-agent allowlists
- [x] Build passes
- [x] Manual: configure hub-spoke agents (david/spoke/kelly), verify per-agent outbound rules — see `Real behavior proof` section below

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: per-agent outbound `agentToAgent.allow` correctly gates cross-agent session tool access. Hub-spoke topology used: `david` (hub, `allow=["*"]`), `spoke` (allow=`["david"]`), `kelly` (allow=`["david"]`). Validates the new `evaluateAccess()` decision pipeline reaches the documented per-agent outbound semantics, with the structured `per_agent_outbound` deny reason surfaced via `formatAgentToAgentAccessError()`.

- **Real environment tested**: Ubuntu 22.04 (kernel 5.15), Node 22.22.0, pnpm 10. Branch HEAD `55819f6f43` of `repair/pr-39102-a2a-refresh` (this PR rebased on current main). Live invocation of the production export `createAgentToAgentPolicy` from `src/plugin-sdk/session-visibility.ts` — no mocks, no test runner, the same code path that backs `sessions_send`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, and `session_status` at runtime.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  1. Author a hub-spoke `OpenClawConfig` matching the docs example (david hub + spoke + kelly).
  2. Build `policy = createAgentToAgentPolicy(cfg)`.
  3. Call `policy.evaluateAccess(requester, target)` for the five edge pairs.
  4. Run with: `pnpm tsx proof-hub-spoke.ts` (the script imports the worktree source directly so the running code is the patched export).

- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output, copied verbatim from `node` stdout via `tsx`.

  ```
  # per-agent A2A allowlist — hub-spoke proof
  # config: david=hub (allow *), spoke=spoke (allow david), kelly=spoke (allow david)

  [ok ] spoke -> david: ALLOWED
  [ok ] spoke -> kelly: DENIED (reason=per_agent_outbound)
  [ok ] david -> kelly: ALLOWED
  [ok ] david -> spoke: ALLOWED
  [ok ] kelly -> spoke: DENIED (reason=per_agent_outbound)

  all cases match expected behavior
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: all five real invocations matched the documented hub-spoke semantics. Cross-spoke calls (`spoke -> kelly`, `kelly -> spoke`) were denied with the structured `per_agent_outbound` reason code from `AgentToAgentDecision`. Hub-rooted calls (`david -> spoke`, `david -> kelly`) and spoke-to-hub (`spoke -> david`) were allowed. Process exit code: `0`. Console output above is the literal stdout, no post-processing.

- **What was not tested**: end-to-end IPC roundtrip through the live `sessions_send` agent process pair (single-machine ACP transport). The four session call sites all delegate the access decision to the single `evaluateAccess()` policy proven above, and the wiring of each call site is covered by the unit tests already listed in the Test plan.

target.title: fix(plugins): stop tool-discovery loads from clearing active providers
target.body:
## Summary

- Tool-discovery plugin loads run with `activate: false` and a tool-only plugin scope, so the registry they build omits provider-only plugins.
- `installStandaloneRegistry` unconditionally called `setActivePluginRegistry`, so those loads replaced the live active registry and wiped its `providers`. The gateway `tools-catalog` channel load behind `agents.list` (and the `resolvePluginTools` cold-load) hit this path, so a bundled provider plugin disappeared at runtime after the first tool-discovery load.
- Fix: a tool-discovery load becomes the active registry **only when none exists yet** (cold start). Once an active registry is present, the tool-only registry is only pinned to its requested `channel`/`http-route` surface, so the live providers are preserved. The cold-start promotion still initializes runtime state (cache key / `workspaceDir`), so later surface lookups hit and the bootstrap path does not reload redundantly.
- Also source plugin tool catalog metadata and declared tools from the active registry, the selected channel registry, and the **raw pinned channel registry**, so a tool-only plugin's display name, description, risk, and tags survive even when the channel selector returns an active registry that has channels. The active registry stays authoritative for duplicate keys.
- Out of scope: normal activation (`activate !== false`) and the migration provider runtime, which intentionally relies on `setActivePluginRegistry` and does not set `toolDiscovery`.
- Reviewers should focus on `installStandaloneRegistry` (cold-start promotion vs pin-only) and the `tools.catalog` metadata merge (active + selected channel + raw pinned channel).

## Linked context

No linked issue; this is a standalone runtime bug fix. Not requested by a maintainer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: After the first tool-discovery load (e.g. a gateway `agents.list` / `tools-catalog` call), `getActivePluginRegistry().providers` dropped to an empty array, so a bundled provider-only plugin vanished from the live runtime even though it was loaded at startup.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw gateway running from a packaged build on Windows, where the defect was first observed through console instrumentation in `setActivePluginRegistry` (active `providers` going from 1 to 0 on the tool-discovery stack). Verified after the patch with a Node reproduction (`tsx`) driving the real loader/runtime source on this branch (Node 22, this checkout).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `npx tsx __repro_provider_clobber.mts` it seeds an active registry that carries a provider-only plugin, then drives the real `ensureStandaloneRuntimePluginRegistryLoaded` channel tool-discovery path that the gateway `tools-catalog` / `agents.list` relies on, and prints the active `providers` count before and after.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): copied live terminal output after the patch:

```text
REPRO providers BEFORE tool-discovery : 1
REPRO providers AFTER  tool-discovery : 1
REPRO active registry was replaced?   : false
REPRO RESULT                          : PROVIDERS PRESERVED
```

- Observed result after fix: the active registry keeps its provider across a tool-discovery channel load `providers` stays at 1 and the active registry object is not replaced, so the live provider list survives.
- What was not tested: a full over-the-wire `agents.list` round-trip inside the packaged gateway binary was not re-run after a full rebuild; provider preservation was confirmed through the Node reproduction and the loader regression coverage instead.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the reproduction drives the real loader/runtime source through `tsx` rather than the fully repackaged gateway binary, because rebuilding and re-syncing the packaged runtime into the host application is out of band for this checkout. The pre-fix symptom itself was captured in the live gateway runtime via console instrumentation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): same script with the patch stashed (guard removed):

```text
REPRO providers BEFORE tool-discovery : 1
REPRO providers AFTER  tool-discovery : 0
REPRO active registry was replaced?   : true
REPRO RESULT                          : PROVIDERS CLEARED
```

<details>
<summary>Reproduction script used above</summary>

```ts
import { createEmptyPluginRegistry } from "./src/plugins/registry-empty.js";
import {
  getActivePluginRegistry,
  resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest,
  setActivePluginRegistry,
} from "./src/plugins/runtime.js";
import { ensureStandaloneRuntimePluginRegistryLoaded } from "./src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.js";

resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest();

const active = createEmptyPluginRegistry();
active.plugins.push({ id: "qclaw", status: "loaded" } as never);
active.providers.push({ id: "qclaw", pluginId: "qclaw" } as never);
setActivePluginRegistry(active, "startup-key", "default", "/tmp/ws");

console.log("REPRO providers BEFORE tool-discovery :", getActivePluginRegistry()?.providers.length);

ensureStandaloneRuntimePluginRegistryLoaded({
  surface: "channel",
  forceLoad: true,
  loadOptions: {
    config: { plugins: { enabled: true } } as never,
    onlyPluginIds: ["__repro_tool_plugin__"],
    activate: false,
    toolDiscovery: true,
    workspaceDir: "/tmp/ws",
  },
});

const after = getActivePluginRegistry();
console.log("REPRO providers AFTER  tool-discovery :", after?.providers.length);
console.log("REPRO active registry was replaced?   :", after !== active);
console.log(
  "REPRO RESULT                          :",
  after?.providers.length ? "PROVIDERS PRESERVED" : "PROVIDERS CLEARED",
);
```

</details>

## Tests and validation

- Focused suites (all green): `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/tools-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts` -> gateway shard 16/16, plugins shard 73/73.
- Types: `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` are clean for the changed files; `git diff --check` is clean.
- Ran the Node reproduction above before and after the patch (see Real behavior proof).
- Regression coverage:
  - loader (`standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts`): a `toolDiscovery` channel load pins the channel surface without replacing an existing active registry; a `toolDiscovery` active load does not replace an existing active registry; a `toolDiscovery` load initializes the active registry when none exists (cold start); a non-`toolDiscovery` active load still installs as the active registry (migration provider path).
  - catalog (`tools-catalog.test.ts`): plugin tool metadata is projected from the pinned channel registry; and from the raw pinned channel registry even when the channel selector returns an active registry that has channels (the active-with-channels + pinned-tool-only case).
- What failed before this fix: the reproduction showed the active `providers` dropping from 1 to 0 (active registry replaced) before the guard was added.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` once an active registry exists, provider-only plugins are no longer wiped from it by tool-discovery loads.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No` the migration provider runtime load has no `toolDiscovery` flag and still installs as the active registry exactly as before.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.
- Highest-risk area: changing when a standalone load becomes globally active (cold-start promotion vs pin-only) and the `tools.catalog` metadata source.
- How is that risk mitigated: full activation (`activate !== false`) and the migration-provider path are unchanged; on a true cold start the promoted tool-only registry is replaced by the first full activation; catalog metadata reads the raw pinned channel registry so tool-only plugins are not hidden by the channel selector; the loader and `tools.catalog` branches are covered by regression tests.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review (the open ClawSweeper P1 below is addressed).
- Copilot review: addressed the load cache that prevents reloads (`setCachedPluginRegistry` / `getReusableCachedPluginRegistry`) is keyed off the load cache key and is independent of `setActivePluginRegistry`, so skipping the active-registry write does not cause extra plugin reloads.
- ClawSweeper P1 (raw pinned registry): addressed `tools.catalog` now merges metadata and declared tools from the raw pinned channel registry (`getPinnedActivePluginChannelRegistry`) in addition to the active and selected channel registries, so a pinned tool-only registry is read even when `getActivePluginChannelRegistry()` selects an active registry that has channels. Added a regression for the active-with-channels + pinned-tool-only case.

target.title: fix(agents): repair retained compaction tool-use pairs
target.body:
Fixes #93321

## Summary

- Repairs compaction history pruning before any budget-driven chunk drop so retained history cannot keep an assistant tool call without a matching tool result.
- Adopts the repaired prune output in the production `compaction-safeguard` caller even when `droppedChunks === 0`, which was the missing caller boundary from the first review.
- Reuses the existing `repairToolUseResultPairing()` transcript repair contract instead of adding provider-specific compaction logic.
- Adds both helper-level and caller-level regressions for the no-prune dangling tool-use path.
- Out of scope: changing provider adapters, replay policy, or compaction summary prompt behavior.

## Linked context

Closes #93321

Related: strict provider validation rejects compacted transcripts with `tool_use ids were found without tool_result blocks` when an assistant tool call survives without a result.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Compaction history pruning could return repaired messages for a no-drop prune plan, but the production compaction-safeguard caller only adopted `pruned.messages` when `droppedChunks > 0`. In the timeout/no-drop case, the caller could still pass `assistant -> user` into summarization/replay and preserve a bare assistant `tool_use`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, latest `origin/main` at `c1219d161d`, branch head `c7952f7ad4`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts -t "uses repaired no-drop prune output for summarization"
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.test.ts src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts
$ OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base origin/main
$ git diff --check
$ node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
$ node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/compaction-planning.ts src/agents/compaction.test.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts
$ gitleaks protect --staged --redact
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts -t "uses repaired no-drop prune output for summarization"
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 190 skipped (192)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  96 passed (96)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.test.ts src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  48 passed (48)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  18 passed (18)

$ OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base origin/main
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
[check:changed] src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts: core test
[check:changed] src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts: core production
[check:changed] src/agents/compaction-planning.ts: core production
[check:changed] src/agents/compaction.test.ts: core test
...
Database-first legacy-store guard passed.
...
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

`git diff --check`, conflict-marker check, targeted `oxfmt --check`, and staged `gitleaks protect --staged --redact` also exited 0.

- Observed result after fix: The caller-level regression drives the real compaction-safeguard handler with a timeout-shaped assistant tool call and enough new-content pressure to request a prune plan, while the retained history itself needs no chunk drop. Before the caller fix, `summarizeInStages` received roles `assistant,user`. After the fix, it receives `assistant,toolResult,user`, with the synthesized `toolResult` matching `toolCallId: "call-timeout"` and `isError: true`.
- What was not tested: No live Anthropic/Bedrock API call was made; the failure is covered at the local compaction-safeguard caller boundary that prepares the retained transcript before provider replay.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This proof uses the actual compaction-safeguard extension handler with mocked auth and summarization output so no external model key or provider account is required. That is the narrow local boundary that creates or prevents the invalid retained transcript shape.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The new caller-level regression failed before the caller fix with this output:

```console
AssertionError: expected [ 'assistant', 'user' ] to deeply equal [ 'assistant', 'toolResult', 'user' ]
```

The helper-level regression also failed on current `main` before the first patch:

```console
AssertionError: expected [ 'assistant', 'user' ] to deeply equal [ 'assistant', 'toolResult', 'user' ]
```

## Tests and validation

- Added helper-level regression coverage in `src/agents/compaction.test.ts` for retained dangling tool-use repair when no chunks are pruned.
- Added caller-level regression coverage in `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` proving the production compaction-safeguard handler adopts repaired no-drop prune output before summarization.
- Passed focused caller regression test: `2` tests across the two selected Vitest shards.
- Passed full compaction-safeguard suite: `96` tests.
- Passed focused compaction and transcript repair tests: `18` + `48` tests.
- Passed `check-changed --base origin/main` for core/coreTests changed lanes.
- Passed formatting, whitespace/conflict-marker, and staged secret checks.

## Duplicate audit

Immediately before pushing the follow-up, I reran five live open-PR searches. Each query only returned this PR, `#93332`, and no separate open PR coverage:

- `Fixes #93321`: only `#93332`
- `#93321`: only `#93332`
- `orphaned tool_use compaction`: only `#93332`
- `tool_result blocks compaction`: only `#93332`
- `compaction tool_use`: only `#93332`

## CI note

Before the follow-up push, the only red check was `checks-node-agentic-commands-agent-channel`, timing out in `src/commands/agent.test.ts` at `does not forward agent-scoped to session selectors as delivery targets`. That test is outside this compaction diff. I reproduced the same local hang with:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent.test.ts -t "does not forward agent-scoped to session selectors as delivery targets"
```

and interrupted it after it exceeded the CI timeout window. I did not change that unrelated command-delivery test in this PR.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` - compaction now repairs retained dangling tool calls instead of preserving an invalid strict-provider transcript.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

What is the highest-risk area? Transcript repair can add a synthetic error tool result to retained compaction history.

How is that risk mitigated? The PR reuses the existing `repairToolUseResultPairing()` behavior already used by replay/session repair paths, adopts the repaired output at the production caller boundary, and covers both helper-level and caller-level no-drop behavior.

## Current review state

Addressed ClawSweeper's P1 review finding by adopting repaired zero-drop prune output in `compaction-safeguard` and adding caller-level regression coverage.

Next action: fresh CI and ClawSweeper re-review.

target.title: fix(agents): persist delivered assistant text [AI]
target.body:
## Summary
- Problem: tool-using embedded-agent turns can deliver final assistant text through `assistantTexts` without a matching durable assistant text message.
- Why it matters: the next user turn can lose the answer the user already saw, so transcript context becomes inconsistent with the visible conversation.
- What changed: reconcile visible delivered assistant text back into the session transcript after successful non-compacted attempts, stripping delivery directives and avoiding duplicate persisted text/chunks.
- Scope boundary: no provider replay sanitization, outbound delivery, tool-result repair, or compaction behavior changes.

Related #77447, #77448.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Delivered assistant text from tool-using embedded-agent turns was visible to the user but could be missing from durable transcript state, so later turns lost context the user had already seen.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS rebased on current `origin/main` at commits `1227486ee2` and `9b2b46f229`, using the repo runner against the actual embedded-runner transcript reconciliation path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-text-persistence.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/usage-reporting.test.ts` and `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the local checkout:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-text-persistence.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/usage-reporting.test.ts
unit-fast: 1 file passed, 8 tests passed
agents: 2 files passed, 152 tests passed
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 27.97s

$ git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: Missing delivered assistant text is appended as durable assistant prose before the next unrelated user turn, while already-persisted text, covered streamed chunks, `MEDIA:` directives, and `NO_REPLY` payloads are not duplicated as visible prose. The attempt keeps the real model `lastAssistant` for accounting: the regression preserves a non-zero `totalTokens=125` usage snapshot while the durable transcript mirror remains zero-usage.
- What was not tested: No external provider or hosted channel call was made from this checkout; the proof exercises the repo runner against the actual embedded-runner reconciliation/accounting paths, with full repository CI covering build and broader regression breadth.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the patch, the durable transcript could remain `user -> assistant(toolCall) -> toolResult -> next user` even though the user had seen a final assistant answer. Before the follow-up fix, the zero-usage transcript mirror could also replace `lastAssistant`, risking bad usage/accounting metadata.

## Root Cause
- `assistantTexts` is the user-visible final text source for some streaming/tool-call flows, but session persistence can already contain only the tool-call assistant/tool-result sequence. Nothing reconciled the delivered final text back into the durable transcript before the next turn.

## Testing
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-text-persistence.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/usage-reporting.test.ts
- git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Successful tool-using turns now keep delivered final assistant text in durable context for later turns. Delivery directives such as `MEDIA:` and silent `NO_REPLY` payloads are not persisted as assistant prose.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

target.title: fix(plugin-runtime): restore channel turn alias
target.body:
## Summary

- Restores the deprecated `core.channel.turn.*` runtime surface as an alias of `core.channel.inbound.*` for shipped channel plugins compiled before the inbound rename.
- Keeps the alias as the same object as `inbound`, so there is no second implementation path or drift in production runtime behavior.
- Updates the PluginRuntime type, plugin runtime mock, API baseline hash, and SDK docs to describe the compatibility alias while keeping new plugin guidance on `inbound.*`.
- Intentionally does not change bundled Feishu source, channel logic, config, or package artifacts.

## Linked context

Closes #91122

Related #91122

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No direct maintainer request; this is a focused compatibility fix for the reported shipped plugin runtime break.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Restores `runtime.channel.turn.*` / `core.channel.turn.*` as a deprecated compatibility alias for old channel plugins while preserving `runtime.channel.inbound.*` as the canonical API.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node 24-compatible repo toolchain, branch `fix/91122-runtime-channel-turn-alias` after this patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the runtime, then ran this Node probe against the generated `dist/runtime-channel-UUbgZd8n.js` chunk:

```console
$ node --input-type=module - <<'NODE'
import { createRuntimeChannel } from './dist/runtime-channel-UUbgZd8n.js';
const channel = createRuntimeChannel({
  providerControl: {},
  createUnifiedProviderSetup: () => ({}),
  ensureRuntimeAuthDeps: () => ({}),
  runtimeAdapters: {},
  createAgentStateDB: () => ({}),
  createAgentStateDBForStorePath: () => ({}),
  registerInboundSession: () => {},
  runWithDispatch: () => {},
});
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  hasTurn: Boolean(channel.turn),
  sameObject: channel.turn === channel.inbound,
  sameRun: channel.turn.run === channel.inbound.run,
  sameBuildContext: channel.turn.buildContext === channel.inbound.buildContext,
  sameDispatchReply: channel.turn.dispatchReply === channel.inbound.dispatchReply,
  sameRunPreparedReply: channel.turn.runPreparedReply === channel.inbound.runPreparedReply,
}));
NODE
```

Evidence after fix:

```console
{"hasTurn":true,"sameObject":true,"sameRun":true,"sameBuildContext":true,"sameDispatchReply":true,"sameRunPreparedReply":true}
```

Observed result after fix: The built runtime exposes `channel.turn`; `channel.turn === channel.inbound`; all four legacy functions are the same function references as the canonical inbound runtime helpers.

What was not tested: I did not install `openclaw@2026.6.x` with `@openclaw/feishu@2026.5.28` or run a live Feishu DM against real credentials.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof validates the exact built runtime compatibility surface that old compiled plugins dereference. It does not prove a full Feishu WebSocket setup because that requires live Feishu credentials and an older published plugin install.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Issue #91122 reports old plugins failing on `core.channel.turn.run()` because the runtime only exposed `channel.inbound.*`.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/runtime-channel.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.artifacts/vitest-cache-91122 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/runtime/types-channel.ts src/plugins/runtime/runtime-channel.ts src/plugins/runtime/runtime-channel.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.ts src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/ui.js build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Regression coverage added or updated:

- `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-channel.test.ts` now asserts deprecated `channel.turn` is wired to `channel.inbound` with object/function identity.
- `src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts` now asserts the test helper exposes the same alias and that inbound overrides are visible through `turn`.

What failed before this fix, if known:

- Old compiled plugins calling `core.channel.turn.run()` could dereference `undefined.run` after the runtime rename removed the `turn` key.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added for the runtime surface and the SDK runtime mock.

Notes:

- A full `pnpm build` rerun reached `tsdown`, runtime postbuild, plugin SDK DTS generation, SDK export checks, and the final `ui:build` stage, then hit my 15 minute local command timeout before completion. I ran the final UI build stage separately with `node scripts/ui.js build`, which passed. An earlier full `pnpm build` completed successfully on this worktree before the final test-helper alias correction.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, old shipped channel plugins can call the deprecated runtime alias again.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Plugin runtime compatibility surface and SDK typing.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The alias reuses the exact same object as `channel.inbound`, avoiding duplicate behavior.
- Type/API baseline, focused runtime tests, plugin runtime mock tests, docs, DTS strict smoke, and autoreview all cover the changed surface.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Ready for ClawSweeper and maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI and ClawSweeper review are pending after the latest branch and proof-body update. Full live Feishu old-plugin validation was not run locally.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local autoreview initially found that the plugin runtime mock used a separate `turn` object; I fixed it so overrides on `inbound` propagate through the alias. Final autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

target.title: Security: add configurable unpaired DM responses
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: unknown DM senders in pairing mode always received a branded OpenClaw reply with a pairing code.
- Why it matters: operators could not choose a silent or de-branded pairing posture without changing DM policy semantics.
- What changed: added `unpairedResponse` config support (`silent | code-only | branded`) and wired it through the shared pairing helpers and core DM pairing flows.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): default behavior remains `branded`, and non-pairing DM policies are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #29945
- Related #29945

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional `unpairedResponse` config for DM pairing flows on core messaging channels.
- `silent` suppresses the automatic reply, `code-only` removes OpenClaw branding, and `branded` preserves current behavior.
- Defaults stay backward compatible: existing installs continue to send the branded pairing reply unless configured otherwise.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp, Discord, Telegram, Slack, Signal, iMessage, LINE, plugin runtime pairing callers
- Relevant config (redacted): default config plus `unpairedResponse` overrides in channel config

### Steps

1. Configure a DM channel with `dmPolicy: pairing`.
2. Send a DM from an unapproved sender.
3. Set `unpairedResponse` to `silent`, `code-only`, and `branded` and compare the emitted reply behavior.

### Expected

- Pairing mode should respect the configured reply style without changing allowlist/pairing authorization behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, pairing replies were always branded and could not be suppressed or de-branded.

target.title: feat(session): add chat-channel session switching with LRU history retention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Chat channels did not support listing or switching between prior sessions under the same SessionKey, and follow-up fixes were needed to preserve switchable transcripts until LRU eviction and restore per-session usage/compaction snapshots correctly.
- Why it matters: Without in-chat session switching, users cannot navigate past conversations from Feishu/DingTalk/Telegram/Discord-style chat surfaces; incorrect transcript/archive or snapshot behavior would also make `/session back`, `/sessions`, and `/status`-adjacent state feel unreliable.
- What changed: Added `/sessions`, `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for chat channels; introduced per-SessionKey LRU `sessionHistory`; preserved switchable transcripts until history eviction; restored token/context/compaction snapshots on switch; documented matching/order/retention behavior; cleaned up reset semantics so `session.historyLimit=0` truly disables switchable history.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change SessionKey routing, does not add cross-SessionKey switching, does not add named sessions/UI picker flows, and does not change channel plugin-specific transport behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #9959
- Related #28168

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Added `/sessions` to list switchable sessions for the current chat SessionKey.
- Added `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for in-chat session switching.
- Current session is included in `/sessions`, marked as `[current]`.
- `/sessions` now shows compact single-line preview rows with session ID prefix and truncated preview text.
- Session switching now restores stored token/context/compaction snapshots for the target session.
- `session.historyLimit` now consistently supports `0` as “disable switchable session history”.
- Transcripts for sessions still in history remain switchable; transcripts are archived when evicted from the LRU history queue.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw dev runtime / source tree
- Model/provider: N/A for core session-switch logic
- Integration/channel (if any): chat-channel command flow; manually validated in a real chat session
- Relevant config (redacted):
  - `session.historyLimit: 5` (default path)
  - `session.historyLimit: 0` (disable-history regression path)

### Steps

1. Start a chat conversation and create multiple sessions with `/new`.
2. Run `/sessions`, then switch via `/session <number>` or `/session back`.
3. Continue creating sessions until the history limit is exceeded.

### Expected

- `/sessions` lists current + recent switchable sessions in LRU order.
- Switching restores the target session’s stored metadata/snapshots.
- Sessions still present in history remain switchable.
- The oldest session is archived only after LRU eviction.
- With `session.historyLimit=0`, switchable history is disabled and the previous session is archived immediately on reset.

### Actual

- Behavior matches the expected results above in targeted tests and manual validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `/sessions` output in chat
  - `/session back` restores the previous session
  - switching to a still-retained historical session restores expected context/state
  - LRU eviction archives only the oldest evicted session
  - daily reset path preserves switchable history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` disables switchable history and archives immediately on reset
- Edge cases checked:
  - history boundary behavior at the configured limit
  - selecting current session is a no-op
  - unique-prefix SessionId selection behavior
  - stale/daily-reset session rollover
- What you did **not** verify:
  - every individual chat provider/plugin surface
  - cross-SessionKey switching (out of scope)
  - any UI/card-based session picker (out of scope)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

  - revert this PR, or set `session.historyLimit=0` to disable switchable session history
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts`

  - `/sessions` shows stale/incorrect ordering
